title: Bakery Website with Menu, Admin Panel, and Rewards Program - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Apply warm artisan bakery visual theme with color palette, typography, and cozy layout across all pages
acceptanceCriteria: All pages share a consistent color palette of warm bakery tones (creams, tans, browns, terracotta)
Typography evokes a handcrafted, artisan feel
Layout feels cozy and inviting throughout

file_operations.description: Define warm bakery color palette CSS custom properties (cream, warm tan, deep brown, soft terracotta) and apply handcrafted-style typography with a cozy, inviting base style
Extend Tailwind theme with bakery color palette (cream, tan, brown, terracotta) and custom font families for artisan typography

requirements.summary: Create public-facing bakery homepage with hero section, navigation bar, and bakery description
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage displays a hero section with bakery name and tagline
Navigation links to Menu and Rewards pages
Brief bakery description is visible on the homepage

file_operations.description: Create homepage with hero section featuring the generated bakery-hero-banner.dim_1400x500.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/bakery-hero-banner.dim_1400x500.png, navigation bar with links to Menu and Rewards pages, and a brief bakery description section
Create navigation bar component with logo using frontend/public/assets/generated/bakery-logo.dim_128x128.png and links to Home, Menu, and Rewards pages, plus login/logout button
Wire HomePage as the default route at '/' and include Navigation component in the app layout

requirements.summary: Build public Menu page displaying available menu items grouped by category with name, description, and price
acceptanceCriteria: Menu page is accessible at a /menu route
Items are grouped by category with clear section headers
Each item card shows name, description, and formatted price
Only items marked as available are shown to the public

file_operations.description: Create public menu page that fetches menu items via getMenuItems backend method, filters for available items only, groups by category (breads, pastries, cakes, drinks), and displays each item with name, description, and formatted price
Create React Query hook to fetch menu items from backend getMenuItems method with proper caching and error handling
Create menu item card component displaying item name, description, price (formatted as currency), and image if available
Register MenuPage at /menu route in the router configuration

requirements.summary: Implement Internet Identity-based admin authentication with role-based access control
acceptanceCriteria: Admin logs in via Internet Identity
Backend validates the caller's principal against a stored admin principal
Admin panel route is inaccessible to non-admin users
Admin can be set as the canister deployer's principal by default

file_operations.description: Use the authorization component to create login/logout button with Internet Identity integration following the component's usage instructions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Use the authorization component to create hooks for getCallerUserProfile and saveCallerUserProfile following the component's usage instructions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Use the authorization component to create modal for first-time user profile setup (name entry) following the component's usage instructions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create React Query hook to check if current user is admin via isCallerAdmin backend method
Create admin guard wrapper component that checks admin status and redirects non-admin users to homepage, showing appropriate unauthorized message

requirements.summary: Build Admin Panel page for managing menu items with add, edit, delete, and availability toggle capabilities
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel lists all menu items with edit and delete buttons
Admin can add a new item via a form with all required fields
Admin can edit any field of an existing item
Admin can toggle an item's availability
Admin can delete an item
All changes are persisted in the backend and reflected immediately on the public menu

file_operations.description: Create admin panel page wrapped with AdminGuard, displaying menu management section and rewards management section in tabs or sections
Create menu management component showing table/list of all menu items with edit and delete actions, plus button to open add item form

Create form component for adding/editing menu items with fields for name, description, price, category dropdown, availability toggle, and imageUrl input. Calls addMenuItem or updateMenuItem backend methods.
Create React Query mutations for addMenuItem, updateMenuItem, and removeMenuItem backend methods with query invalidation to refresh menu data
Register AdminPanelPage at /admin route in the router configuration
Add conditional Admin link in navigation bar visible only when user is authenticated and isAdmin

requirements.summary: Build public Rewards page showing logged-in customer's rewards status and progress to next free treat
acceptanceCriteria: Rewards page is accessible at a /rewards route
Unauthenticated users see a prompt to log in with Internet Identity
Authenticated users see their total items purchased count
Authenticated users see how many free treats they have available
A progress indicator shows how many more items are needed to earn the next free treat (e.g., 3 more items to go)

file_operations.description: Create rewards page that shows login prompt for unauthenticated users, or displays customer rewards status (items purchased, free treats available, progress to next reward) for authenticated users via getCustomerRewards backend method
Create React Query hook to fetch customer rewards data via getCustomerRewards backend method, with mutation for claimFreeTreat
Create component displaying customer rewards statistics with progress bar showing items until next free treat based on rewards config
Register RewardsPage at /rewards route in the router configuration

requirements.summary: Add admin section to configure rewards program rule and manually adjust customer rewards
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can view and update the 'items per free treat' threshold (default 5)
Changes to the threshold apply to future reward calculations
Admin can search a customer by principal and view their rewards record
Admin can manually add or subtract free treats for a customer

file_operations.description: Create rewards management component for admin panel with form to update rewards config (items per free treat) via updateRewardsConfig, and customer lookup/adjustment form using getCustomerRewardsByPrincipal and adjustCustomerRewards backend methods
Create React Query hook to fetch rewards config via getRewardsConfig backend method with mutation for updateRewardsConfig
Create React Query mutations for adjustCustomerRewards and recordPurchase backend methods
Integrate RewardsManagement component into admin panel layout alongside MenuManagement

requirements.summary: Add 'Record Purchase' flow in admin panel to update customer rewards balance
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel has a 'Record Purchase' form with a principal input and item count input
Submitting the form calls the backend to update that customer's rewards
Success confirmation is shown after recording a purchase

file_operations.description: Create form component with principal text input and item count number input, calling recordPurchase backend method on submit with success/error feedback
Integrate RecordPurchaseForm component into the rewards management section of the admin panel

requirements.summary: Wire generated bakery-themed images into site header and homepage hero
acceptanceCriteria: Hero banner image is displayed on the homepage hero section
Logo/icon appears in the navigation bar
Images are served from frontend/public/assets/generated

file_operations.description: Reference hero banner image from frontend/public/assets/generated/bakery-hero-banner.dim_1400x500.png in the hero section background or img element
Reference logo image from frontend/public/assets/generated/bakery-logo.dim_128x128.png in the navigation bar header
